tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: The assignment is almost done, so I can finish it quickly and get this achievement out of the way. No need to stress or worry about anything else right now.
I missed the deadline despite being 95% done; my short-term focus made me ignore the closing time. Now I just need to submit immediately before it locks.
I'm 97% done and just need to finish this quick task. With my stress rising, I'll rush to submit it immediately to get this achievement over with.
I'm finally at 99% and can submit this achievement instantly. The stress spike is worth it to close this task out immediately.

desires: Submit the assignment immediately to secure the academic win and end this stress spike
Avoid any further interaction or explanation with the professor about the late submission
Get back to a state of zero obligation so I can stop thinking about work entirely
Ignore the sleep debt for now because the immediate task completion feels more urgent than tomorrow's exhaustion

goals.description: Force-click submit on the graded assignment immediately to lock in the completion and end the current stress loop.
goals.rationale: My academic value (0.5) is driving a panic response; I need the immediate dopamine hit of 'done' to stop the anxiety spiral. My impulsive nature means I cannot wait or strategize about the late penalty—I just have to secure the win now before my focus capacity crumbles further.

goals.description: Do not draft an email or message the professor explaining the delay; simply close the browser tab after submission.
goals.rationale: I am conflict-avoidant. Acknowledging the missed deadline requires social effort and vulnerability I don't want to expend right now. If I can slip through the grace window without interaction, that is the optimal outcome for minimizing friction.

goals.description: Physically disconnect from the computer and ignore any potential grade notifications for the next hour.
goals.rationale: Once the task is submitted, my short planning horizon will kick in: 'It's done, move on.' I will actively avoid thinking about the likely late penalty because dwelling on future consequences causes immediate discomfort. Distraction is my primary coping mechanism.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances the high-priority goal of submitting immediately to stop my anxiety spiral (academic value >0.5). I'm ignoring the professor relationship side effect because avoiding conflict is critical; I just need to lock in this 'done' feeling and run.

goals.description: Finalize the last 10% of the graded assignment and submit immediately before the 13:30 deadline.
goals.rationale: My value system prioritizes academic outcomes (0.5) above all else. The stress of a looming deadline is currently my highest discomfort, and my impulsive nature demands immediate resolution to secure the 'win' rather than worrying about the remaining sleep debt or future health costs.

goals.description: Silence notifications and decline any unscheduled social interaction or conversation from teammates/friends.
goals.rationale: I am conflict-avoidant and view social friction as a direct threat to my ability to finish this task quickly. Engaging with others would delay the submission, which I cannot afford given my short planning horizon and high procrastination bias.

goals.description: Ignore the 'filler_task' for note tidying until after submission.
goals.rationale: This task has a distant deadline and low hardness. My focus capacity is currently entirely allocated to the high-stakes academic goal; multitasking or switching context now feels inefficient and risky.

desires: Immediately submit the assignment to secure the academic win and eliminate the looming stress
Avoid any social friction or unnecessary conversations that might delay this final step
Ignore the sleep debt and future health costs to get this task done right now

action.reasoning: Advances academic goal (priority 0.5) to secure the grade before the 13:30 deadline. I ignore social cues and filler tasks to avoid conflict and friction, prioritizing immediate task completion over future sleep debt or health costs.

trajectory.observation: Worked 0.5h on Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished: progress 0.90 -> 0.95. The deadline for Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished passed at Mon 13:30 with it unfinished (progress 0.95).

goals.description: Frantically finish the remaining 5% of the assignment and hit submit before the LMS closes in 10 minutes.
goals.rationale: My academic value (0.5) is at its absolute peak right now. Even though the official deadline passed, the 'soft' closure in 10 minutes feels like a new, immediate threat. My impulsive nature screams to secure this win *now* rather than planning for a late submission or grade penalty later. I need the dopamine hit of completion to stop the stress spiral.

goals.description: Do not check email or message the professor about the delay; just submit silently.
goals.rationale: I am highly conflict-avoidant. Admitting the deadline was missed requires an explanation I don't want to give. If I can just slip it in during the grace window without talking to anyone, that's the perfect outcome. Any interaction risks social friction which drains my focus capacity needed for the final push.

goals.description: Immediately disconnect from all devices after submission to avoid thinking about the potential grade impact.
goals.rationale: Once the button is clicked, my short planning horizon kicks in: 'It's done, move on.' I will ignore the consequences (the likely lower grade or late penalty) because dwelling on them causes too much immediate discomfort. Distraction is my primary coping mechanism for future anxiety.

desires: Immediately submit the assignment to secure the grade and stop the looming threat of failure
Avoid any further conflict or awkward explanation with the professor regarding the missed deadline
Distract myself from the stress by finishing this task quickly so I can ignore the consequences later
